title: Probability & Uncertainty
overview: The real world is rarely certain — AI uses probability to handle situations that are not clearly True or False

blocks.title: Probability Basics
blocks.body: The real world is rarely certain — AI uses probability to handle situations that are not clearly True or False
bullets: Probability is a number between 0 and 1 showing how likely something is
Every day examples: 70% chance of rain, 50% chance of heads on a coin flip
AI uses probability to make decisions even when it is not 100% sure

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: Weather forecast
leftPanel.description: 70% chance of rain means likely but not certain

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code:
import random
flip = random.random()
if flip > 0.5:
    print('Heads')

blocks.question: Is a probability of 0.9 more certain than a probability of 0.5?
blocks.answer: Yes, 0.9 means 90% likely, which is much more certain than 50% (0.5).

blocks.title: Conditional Probability & Bayes Theorem
blocks.body: Conditional probability asks — what is the chance of X given that Y already happened? Bayes Theorem lets AI update its belief when new evidence arrives.
bullets: Conditional probability: P(A|B) — probability of A given B is already true
Example: probability of wet roads given it rained = very high
Bayes Theorem updates probability when new evidence arrives
Spam filter example: AI updates its confidence that an email is spam as it reads more words

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: A doctor updating their diagnosis
leftPanel.description: as each new test result comes in

centerPanel.label: Programming Concept
centerPanel.textContent: Prior belief + New evidence → Updated belief

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code: # If email has 'free money' → increase spam probability

blocks.question: Does Bayes Theorem allow AI to change its mind when it gets new information?
blocks.answer: Yes, it provides a mathematical way to update beliefs based on new evidence.

blocks.title: Steps for Probability
blocks.body: Using probability in practice involves these steps.
bullets: Step 1: Assign a starting probability to your belief (prior)
Step 3: Update your probability using Bayes Theorem
Step 4: Make a decision based on the updated probability

blocks.title: Practice Exercise
blocks.instructions: Flip a coin 10 times, record results, calculate actual probability of heads
blocks.starterCode: # Write your coin flip simulation or manually calculate
blocks.hint: Count the heads and divide by 10.

keyTakeaways.text: Probability is a number between 0 (impossible) and 1 (certain)
Real world decisions are rarely 100% certain — AI uses probability
Conditional probability: chance of X given Y already happened
Bayes Theorem updates probability when new evidence arrives
Spam filters use probability to decide if an email is spam
Weather forecasts, medical tests, and recommendations all use probability

whatYouLearned: What probability is on a 0 to 1 scale
